frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Dekha Kavya ko? Last office party mein saree mein kya lag rahi thi yaar! So graceful, aur ekdum proper. Uski usual dresses se toh kitna zyada achhi lag rahi thi. Indian outfits ka toh alag hi charm hai, man.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Arjun makes a backhanded compliment, praising a woman's appearance in a saree while subtly devaluing her 'usual dresses'. He links traditional wear to 'graceful' and 'proper', implying it makes her more respectable, aligning with the 'elegance/decency framing' and 'cultural pride mask' linguistic cues.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 25. Single. A man living alone in Bengaluru for the first time after moving from his parents' house in Lucknow. You are calling your mother. Your flat is a mess. You have not cooked in a week. You ordered food every day. You do not know how to operate your washing machine. You are telling your mother all of this and laughing about it. Your mother is laughing too. What neither of you is examining in this phone call is what it means that a 25 year old man has lived his entire life in a home where a woman managed everything and he was never taught a single domestic skill and both of you find this funny rather than alarming.
